role_summary: Civil rights movement participant; co-subject of a joint oral history with Jeanette Smith (catalog entry 100, publication-block tier).
ai_reading: Raylawni G. Branch shares the embedding signature of the joint oral history at entry 100 (catalog slug 'raylawni-g-branch-and-jeanette-smith'); see that catalog page for the substrate-derived neighbor cluster and axis observations the embedding produces for the combined transcript. Individual cosines for each named subject of a joint interview are not separable from the precomputed substrate, since the LoC transcript was recorded as a single audio file with multiple voices interleaved.
biographical_paragraph: Raylawni G. Branch is one of the named subjects of the joint Civil Rights History Project oral history at entry 100 (American Folklife Center accession AFC 2010/039) [src: 1]. This catalog page exists as a per-person index for Raylawni G. Branch; the substrate signature lives on the parent joint-interview page (see the AI's reading above) since the LoC transcript treats the joint conversation as a single recording. Independent biographical sourcing for Raylawni G. Branch is pending.

sources.title: Joint oral history at entry 100
sources.publisher: Civil Rights History Project, Library of Congress (AFC 2010/039)

gallery.alt: University of Southern Mississippi campus in Hattiesburg
gallery.caption: The University of Southern Mississippi in Hattiesburg, which Branch and Gwendolyn Armstrong became the first African American students to enter on September 6, 1965.
